edibility: Toxicity disputed; many sources list as inedible or poisonous due to reported gastrointestinal effects. Best avoided due to inconsistent toxicity reports and lack of culinary value.
taste: Mild and unremarkable flavor. The flesh blackens immediately upon handling or bruising, which is a distinctive characteristic.
search: Found in old grasslands, meadows, and churchyards throughout temperate regions worldwide, fruiting summer through autumn. Key identification features: conical cap that bruises and ages jet-black; red or orange coloring when young. Similar species include other Hygrocybe species; distinguish by cap shape and blackening tendency.

toxicity.symptoms: Reports of gastrointestinal distress including nausea and stomach upset in some individuals; toxicity not consistently demonstrated across all sources
toxicity.antidote: Supportive care including hydration and rest; consult poison control in case of severe symptoms
